title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in the Boston Public Garden, surpassing its typical 30-50 year lifespan.
No injuries reported when the tree fell; Friends of the Public Garden deemed it structurally sound in prior inspections.
January's removal of another willow followed safety concerns; routine tree care includes pruning and disease management.

title: These new speed boards in Jamaica Plain, on the Esplanade track cyclists’ speeds, not just cars
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New speed boards in Jamaica Plain and Charles River Esplanade monitor cyclist speeds and encourage slower traffic.
The Department of Conservation and Recreation installed the boards after residents expressed concerns about speeding cyclists.
Speed boards were also set up in several locations, including Cambridge's Memorial Drive and Cape Cod Rail Trail, to focus on cyclists.

title: Thousands demand Wu address street safety after cyclist killed in crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 4,000 people signed a letter urging Boston Mayor Michelle Wu to address street safety after Louisa Gag's death.
A vigil was held for Louisa Gag at City Hall Plaza; her death spurred action and commitments from Mayor Wu.
Mayor Wu announced increased enforcement against traffic violations and assigned staff to expedite safety projects.

title: Police investigating after man, woman in their 70s found dead in Maine home
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Maine State Police investigating the deaths of Carolyn Harris, 77, and Kevin Harris, 75, found in West Bath home.
Sagadahoc County Sheriff's Department discovered the deceased during a welfare check on July 13.
Autopsies are being conducted; police state there is no public danger related to the incident.

title: Multiple issues after getting an SUV back from the body shop
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2024 Honda CR-V owner experiences interior and exterior lighting issues after body shop repairs.
Recalibration of the ADAS system might be necessary to resolve the Honda CR-V's programming glitches.
Contacting the insurance company may be required to open a supplemental claim for further repairs.

title: Officers shot at during Dorchester traffic stop, police say, prompting outcry from union
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Boston police officers were shot at during a traffic stop in Dorchester; no injuries were reported.
20-year-old Rasiel Joel Carbuccia was arrested after fleeing; a ghost gun was recovered by police.
The Boston Police Patrolmen's Association called on officials to condemn the attack on the officers.

title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a minivan crash on I-495 North in Andover.
The crash involved a minivan and a sedan; the minivan driver and three children were hospitalized.
State Police continue to investigate the crash, which resulted in a minivan fire and sedan damage.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management purchased 149-175 Harvard Ave., raising rents and prompting three longtime tenants to close or relocate.
The Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo cite higher rents as the reason for leaving the Allston building.
Alpha Management plans renovations and tenant relocations while concerns rise over neighborhood's changing business landscape.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Naismith Basketball Hall of Fame as a coach.
Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett will present Rivers at the Hall of Fame ceremony.
The ceremony is scheduled for August 15 at Symphony Hall in Springfield.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed it feels 'weird' that Jaylen Brown, his longtime teammate, has been traded to the 76ers.
The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
The trade of Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia sparked mixed emotions among Celtics fans and emotional responses on social media.
